source: MDC Google Drive document / Pulse survey tab

surveys.title: Employee pre-pulse
surveys.description: Baseline reflection before practising the CARE framework.

questions.text: I feel comfortable discussing feedback with my manager.
I feel safe sharing my thoughts during feedback conversations.
I sometimes feel defensive when receiving feedback.
I clearly understand feedback given to me.
Feedback I receive is specific and actionable.
I understand how to improve after receiving feedback.
I can reflect on feedback without confusion or misinterpretation.
I apply feedback to improve my work performance.
Feedback helps me improve my performance over time.
Overall, I am satisfied with the feedback culture in my workplace.

surveys.title: Employee post-pulse
surveys.description: Reflection after practising the CARE framework.

questions.text: I feel more comfortable discussing feedback with my manager after the CARE framework.
I feel safer sharing my thoughts during feedback conversations.

questions.sourceNote: Marked reverse in the source, but phrased positively.
questions.text: I feel less defensive when receiving feedback.
I understand feedback more clearly after the CARE framework.
Feedback I receive is more specific and actionable now.
I can reflect on feedback more effectively.
I better understand how to improve after feedback.
I apply feedback more effectively in my work.
Feedback has a stronger impact on my performance now.
Overall, the CARE framework improved my feedback experience.

surveys.title: Manager pre-pulse
surveys.description: Baseline reflection before practising the REAL framework.

questions.text: I identify specific employee behaviours when giving feedback.
I link feedback to clear performance standards or expectations.
I provide clear and actionable improvement steps.
I connect feedback to employee growth or long-term development.
I sometimes avoid giving feedback due to discomfort or fear of reaction.
Overall, I am satisfied with the current feedback culture.

surveys.title: Manager post-pulse
surveys.description: Reflection after practising the REAL framework.

questions.text: I now identify employee behaviours more clearly when giving feedback.
I better link feedback to performance standards.
I provide more actionable improvement steps.
I connect feedback more clearly to employee growth and development.

questions.sourceNote: Marked reverse in the source, but phrased positively.
questions.text: I feel less avoidant when giving feedback.
The REAL framework improved my feedback delivery.
Overall, my feedback conversations are more effective now.
